qa_pairs.question: What is QGIS?
qa_pairs.answer: QGIS is a powerful open-source software designed for visualizing and processing geographic information systems (GIS) data.

qa_pairs.question: How can I launch QGIS within JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: Loading a `qgis` module will add a launcher to start the QGIS graphical user interface through a VNC session in JupyterLab.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of software is Star-CCM+?
qa_pairs.answer: Star-CCM+ is a commercial computational fluid dynamic simulation software developed by Siemens.

qa_pairs.question: How do I launch Star-CCM+ in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: You can launch Star-CCM+ by loading one of the `starccm` or `starccm-mixed` modules, which will add a launcher to start its graphical user interface through a VNC session.

qa_pairs.question: Is a license required to use Star-CCM+?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you will need to provide your own license to use Star-CCM+, as it is a commercial package.

qa_pairs.question: Which scientific Python packages require the `scipy-stack` module to be loaded before opening a notebook?
qa_pairs.answer: If your notebook requires `ipython`, `ipython_genutils`, `ipykernel`, `ipyparallel`, `matplotlib`, `numpy`, `pandas`, or `scipy`, you must load the `scipy-stack` module.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load the `scipy-stack` module in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: You must load the `scipy-stack` module from the JupyterLab `Softwares` tool before you open your notebook.

qa_pairs.question: What is an alternative method to install Python packages directly within a notebook cell?
qa_pairs.answer: You can install needed packages by running the command `pip install --no-index package-name` inside a notebook cell.

qa_pairs.question: Are package installations in the default Python kernel environment persistent across JupyterLab sessions?
qa_pairs.answer: No, installations of packages in the default Python kernel environment are temporary to the lifetime of the JupyterLab session; you will have to reinstall them each time you start a new session.

qa_pairs.question: How can I set up a persistent Python environment in JupyterLab?
qa_pairs.answer: For a persistent Python environment, you must configure a custom Python kernel.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to open an existing Python notebook?
qa_pairs.answer: To open an existing Python notebook, go back to the File Browser, browse to the location of the `.ipynb` file, and double-click on it.

qa_pairs.question: What happens when an existing Python notebook file is opened?
qa_pairs.answer: Double-clicking an existing Python notebook file will open it in a new JupyterLab tab, and an IPython kernel will start running in the background for this notebook.

qa_pairs.question: How can I open a new Python notebook in the current File Browser directory?
qa_pairs.answer: To open a new Python notebook, click on the `Python 3.x` launcher under the `Notebook` section.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step to convert a Jupyter notebook to a Python script?
qa_pairs.answer: From the console, or in a new notebook cell, you need to install `nbconvert` using `!pip install --no-index nbconvert`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I convert a Jupyter notebook file into a Python script?
qa_pairs.answer: You can convert your notebook to a Python script by executing `!jupyter nbconvert --to python my-current-notebook.ipynb`.

qa_pairs.question: How should a converted Python notebook script be run within a non-interactive submission script?
qa_pairs.answer: In your non-interactive submission script, run your converted notebook with the command `python my-current-notebook.py`.

qa_pairs.question: What command is used to submit a non-interactive job containing a converted Python script?
qa_pairs.answer: You can submit your non-interactive job using `sbatch my-submit.sh`.

qa_pairs.question: What is a common error encountered when trying to install packages in a Jupyter notebook?
qa_pairs.answer: A common error is `OSError: [Errno 30] Read-only file system`, which occurs when `pip` tries to uninstall a package in a read-only location.

qa_pairs.question: How can I address the 'Read-only file system' error when installing packages in a notebook cell?
qa_pairs.answer: For many cases, you can use `pip install --no-index --ignore-installed <package>` in your notebook cell to install the package that caused the error.

qa_pairs.question: Does the `--ignore-installed` option always work to fix package installation errors related to a read-only file system?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the `--ignore-installed` option may not work for all packages, such as `pyarrow`, `opencv`, or `mpi4py`.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for questions regarding package installation issues?
qa_pairs.answer: In case of questions, please contact technical support.
